description: Persistent issue tracker. Decider appends new issues from Curmudgeon reviews, marks resolved when wins.json no longer contains the flagged text.

issues.description: CAT-005 proposal: Analyst proposes new section "V51.1 Refraction Cascade: How One Parameter Fix Breaks Everything" covering 12+ affected WINs. Cascade failure analysis shows refraction needed is 13-27 degrees vs 0.03 degrees real. Human must decide: approve as new section (Part 2 or Section 7 extension), reject, or integrate as individual WIN patches.
issues.human_decision: Approved as new section under The Model tab (option a). New code_analysis sub-category tag requested for cascade-affected WINs. Analyst to write recommended proposal package — route to curmudgeon before fleshing out prose. See CAT-005.json human_decision block.
issues.gate_returned_reason: Curmudgeon gate (2026-04-10): ACCEPT_WITH_MODIFICATIONS — 7 blockers. Analyst must produce EXP-050-numerics.json, re-audit WIN flip table (per-WIN re-read required), withdraw cascade_dependent tag, use structural title, renumber to Section 1.8. Resubmit for second gate pass.

issues.description: External report #1 (Devilwench): Positive feedback only. No error claim, correction, or scientific content. "You're awesome don't ever change." No changes needed.
issues.fix_details: Not a problem report — positive feedback only. Analyst logged as reader engagement data point.

issues.description: EXP-060 PIPELINE: generate-html.js needs support for kill-shots.json data file to resolve {{KS_TOTAL}}, {{KS_STRAW_MAN_COUNT}}, {{KS_WAIT_AND_SEE_COUNT}} template placeholders in Part 5 HTML. data/kill-shots.json already created (6 entries). EXP-060 replacement_html uses these placeholders but they are currently unresolved. Current Part 5 uses EXP-057 prose (hardcoded). When pipeline supports it, swap EXP-057 HTML for EXP-060 HTML to get data-driven count injection.
issues.notes: Not urgent — existing Part 5 HTML is complete and correct via EXP-057. This is a refactoring to data-driven counts. Lower priority than content work.

issues.description: SEC-2b.7 (Code Analysis / Scoreboard Split): Curmudgeon review flags that the W004/WIN-025 identity mapping is unverified. The section claims W004 in results.json IS the 2024 Eclipse 9-Station test (WIN-025). But the dome's wins page defines W004 as "Noise Floor Limit" (empirical recalibration). The dome API endpoints (api/results.json) are returning 404 per latest poll — blocking direct verification. The section's core "two scoreboards disagree on the same observation" argument depends on this mapping being correct. If W004 in results.json is NOT the eclipse test, the argument fails.
issues.notes: API endpoint api/results.json returning 404 as of 2026-04-12 poll. Alternative: fetch results.json from dome github repo raw URL. Analyst should: (1) Fetch results.json from GitHub raw, (2) find W004 entry title, (3) if title IS eclipse test, document the W-number namespace inconsistency in section text. If title is NOT eclipse test, rewrite section 2b.7 with correct W-number.

issues.description: FAIL-004 (Section 6.5): Curmudgeon identified two stronger arguments not yet in the text: (1) Two-threshold discrepancy — dome has different criteria for same phenomenon: W020 weekly prediction uses "500hPa ≥3% above climatology" while Live Power Dashboard Domain 10 uses "AAO < 0σ while SAA decay >50 nT/yr" — threshold inconsistency is itself evidence of post-hoc adjustment; (2) Selective window-length rule — new 3-month minimum window applies only to atmospheric predictions (which fail) not magnetic ones (which pass) — asymmetric methodology by prediction type.
issues.notes: Both arguments verified in raw-text/08-live-power.txt (Domain 10 criteria) and raw-text/03-predictions.txt (W020). Adding these would strengthen the goalpost-moving argument significantly. Suggest expansion item if analyst has capacity.

issues.description: Dome site ai-context page returning 404 as of 2026-04-12 poll (was previously accessible). This may affect our review if we link to that page, and could affect SEO/discoverability if our llms.txt references it. Check whether our site links to this page and update if needed. Social agent should check llms.txt for affected references.
issues.notes: Low priority per poll note. Social agent to check llms.txt. Also check docs/index.html for any links to john09289.github.io/ai-context or similar.
